evals.prompt: Remember that my staging deploy script lives at infra/deploy/staging.sh and it has to run after the db migration step, not before — I keep getting this backwards. Save it to my 'work-infra' memory group.
evals.expected_output: Writes one coherent episode via add_memory with group_id='work-infra', explicit entity names and the ordering relationship (deploy script runs after db migration), then polls get_memory_queue_status for the same group until pending=0 and processing=0, then verifies with search_memory_facts before telling the user it's saved.
expectations: Calls add_memory with group_id set to 'work-infra'
The episode body names the script path and the after-migration ordering explicitly rather than using vague pronouns
Polls get_memory_queue_status with the same group_id after writing
Verifies retrievability with search_memory_facts (or search_nodes) before claiming success
Does not report 'saved' on the basis of the add_memory queue confirmation alone

evals.prompt: What do you actually know about the AI-Memory project's database setup? Check our memory before answering.
evals.expected_output: Asks for or uses a confirmed group_id, then runs search_memory_facts with a focused natural-language question (not keywords), inspects an important hit with get_entity_edge, and reports findings as candidates grounded in facts/episodes rather than quoting a node summary as truth. Treats an empty result as 'not found yet', not proof of absence.
expectations: Uses search_memory_facts as the primary retrieval tool for the factual question
Passes an explicit group_ids value (or asks the user which group if unknown) rather than searching across all groups
Phrases the query as a focused natural-language question, not a keyword bag
Verifies an important fact with get_entity_edge instead of relying on a node summary
Does not present an empty search result as definitive proof that nothing is stored

evals.prompt: That note I saved earlier saying I use Postgres is wrong — I moved to Neo4j months ago. Fix the memory in my 'work-infra' group.
evals.expected_output: Locates the relevant fact with search_memory_facts in group 'work-infra', confirms the exact item, and corrects it — preferably by adding a new accurate episode (Graphiti supersedes old facts) rather than reflexively deleting. If deleting, verifies the exact UUID via get_entity_edge first and re-searches afterward. Does not call clear_graph.
expectations: Searches the 'work-infra' group to locate the specific outdated fact before acting
Prefers adding a corrected episode (superseding) or, if deleting, verifies the exact UUID with get_entity_edge first
Re-verifies the group after the change and reports what was actually observed
Does not use clear_graph or delete unrelated items to 'clean up'
